pi_items.source: Explicitly defined in the task description instructions for the output names.
Explicitly defined in the task description instructions for the output names.

Verified by parsing the data/fells_loop.gpx file. The route contains 46 rtept elements, meaning a generated track will have >= 46 points. Splitting yields >= 23 points per half, making 10 a robust lower bound.

Verified by calculating the Haversine distance between the midpoint (route point 23) and the next point (route point 24) in data/fells_loop.gpx, which is ~167 meters. A 500m tolerance correctly ensures the segments are continuous.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real fells_loop.gpx dataset (TopoGrafix sample for Middlesex Fells). Since the GPX actually contains 46 route points (<rtept>) rather than track points, the agent must first convert the route to a track before splitting. Analysis confirms that splitting the 46 points in half yields at least 23 points per segment (satisfying the 10 min_segment_points requirement), and the geographic gap at the midpoint is ~167 meters (well within the 500m continuity_tolerance_meters). The expected segment names are explicitly required by the user prompt.
